dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Patients received 60 sessions of tDCS treatment over 12 weeks, with follow-up assessments conducted 3 months after the primary endpoint.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1: Attexis and tDCS (verum); Arm 2: Attexis and placebo-tDCS (sham).
studies.collected_data: Compliance (number of days of tDCS treatment), efficacy measures (ASRS-v1.1 total score, subscale attention, subscale hyperactivity), stability of effects (change in ASRS-v1.1 sumscore), usability (UEQ sumscore).

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received access to attexis in addition to treatment as usual (TAU). Control group received access to TAU only.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: ADHD Symptom Severity (ASRS v1.1, score range 0-72, higher scores indicate worse outcome) assessed at 3 months. Secondary Outcomes: Depressive Symptomatology (PHQ-9, score range 0-27, higher scores indicate worse outcome), Self Esteem (Rosenberg Self Esteem Scale, score range 0-30, higher scores indicate better outcome), Functioning (Work and Social Adjustment Scale, score range 0-40, higher scores indicate worse outcome), Health-Related Quality of Life (AQoL-8D, score range 0-100, higher scores indicate better outcome), ADHD Symptom Severity (ASRS v1.1) assessed at 6 months.

studies.dtx_name: attexis - die digitale Therapie bei ADHS im Erwachsenenalter
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received attexis + TAU; control group received TAU alone.
studies.collected_data: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale total score, functional impairment, depressive symptoms, self-esteem, health-related quality of life.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Significantly lower ADHD symptom severity in the intervention group at 3 months (baseline-adjusted mean difference = -5.0 points; d = 0.85, p < .001). Significant improvements across all secondary outcomes, effects stable at 6 months. Responder analyses confirmed clinical relevance. Consistent effects across sex, medication use, psychotherapy status, and treatment changes. No adverse events reported.
